channel.title: Iterative Thinking
channel.description: Design, AI, and systems thinking by Ulises Arvizu

item.title: Are you a designer... only when you design?
item.link: https://iterativethinking.substack.com/p/Are you a designer... only when you design?
item.guid: https://iterativethinking.substack.com/p/Are you a designer... only when you design?
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few weeks ago, this article by Lowell Stevens motivated my friend Adrián Solca to make this great video, explaining why UX Design has more to do with other things than just designing a screen. His video reminded me of the paradigm from my university years, where people believed that to study graph…

item.title: Communicate and Share
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I've always found the debate between designers who like to create hype and be protagonists, and those who prefer to stay at the edge of their work.

item.title: When Designers Become Robots
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Lately in my inbox, the multiple newsletters I'm subscribed to have been talking about artificial intelligence, automation, etc. How far does the algorithm know you and suggest things to you, or does it mold you, pulling you into a tunnel of bias?

item.title: Uncertainty and trust in the design process
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I can't deny the fear I feel sitting in front of this blank page every week after sending the newsletter. What's the topic I'll cover in the next edition? — I ask myself. There's an internal voice telling me I should trust, the words and ideas will connect, it's just a matter of dealing with that un…

item.title: Productivity in Remote Times
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few hours ago I had an interesting reflection with my team that made it clear what I needed to write about in this edition. The reality for a little over a year and a half has pushed us toward remote workplaces (in most cases). Celebrated by some; since in Mexico City, where I'm writing this post …

item.title: Metaverse and NFTs, things that make me realize I'm getting old.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: The hype around the latest news about Facebook (now Meta) and their new approach to what the next generation of internet should be, an immersive internet, makes me feel old. On one hand, I'm excited about a ton of possibilities, but on the other it makes me think about the risks these types of exper…

item.title: Things I've learned leading a design team.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Disclaimer: I'm no longer leading a team, I'm now an individual contributor but I want to add to the conversation.

item.title: What to Do When You Have a 1:1?
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: 1:1 meetings (read One to one) are among the most enriching things, in every sense of the word, that I've experienced.

item.title: On design, tasks and outcomes
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few weeks ago I was thinking about my process and its scope. When working on a design systems team, one of the deliverables is the documentation of the designed component.

item.title: Designing 10 Thousand Years Into the Future
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few months ago I watched the Dune movie, and although I knew the work by references, I had never sat down to read it (I just finished the book a few days ago). The film struck me as majestic and visually compelling—the production design is top-notch. You feel like you're in another universe, a wor…

item.title: Before 2021 Goes
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: 2021 is about to end, and we've reached edition 11 of this newsletter, 11 of 52 editions which is the goal I set when I decided to jump into this project. The fact that it's the last week of the year doesn't stop me from writing.

item.title: The Service of Cancellation
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few days ago, I wanted to cancel an insurance policy I had through an Amex card. I called, they verified me as a client, and they simply said "very well sir, we'll proceed with your cancellation, but before we do I'd like to tell you a few things…" Less than 5 minutes and the person on the phone h…

item.title: Time, a Key Constraint in Design
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I'm starting the book Homo Deus, this second part (I haven't read the first one, which is Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind, hopefully that's not a problem) interested me because the author tries to at least sketch paths about the future and technology.

item.title: Design as a craft
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I remember I was always curious about computers, I learned to use Photoshop just by clicking around, version 5.0 and on PC in case you were wondering. When I learned digital editing, what is now basically how all design in the world is done, I wanted to conquer the world.

item.title: UI is not UX
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few years ago, my perspective on design led me to somewhat disdain execution. I'm a visual designer and communicator, so UI execution was my entry point into this world; however, I let myself be influenced by a perspective where execution wasn't the important thing, because, I came to think, it's …

item.title: Learning from NFTs: The Culture of Future Promises
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Disclaimer: Just as an update and explanation for why there was no newsletter for three weeks: My family and I caught COVID, after almost two years of not contracting it, luck wasn't on our side. Fortunately it just meant staying home following the doctor's instructions. We're still in that post-COV…

item.title: People-Centered Organizations
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: One of the maxims when we talk about product or service design is the mindset of being people-centered. This might seem obvious, and even if it's a bit of hype, we all like to say that when we design, we do it putting the user/client/person at the center.

item.title: For a designer, new things always excite me... Web3
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: It was summer 2008, damn nearly 14 years ago, the iPhone was that gadget everyone was talking about, my friend Héctor Zárate had immersed himself during his college summer to learn the programming language to make his first app, Ubicate!, an application that created routes to get from point "A" to p…

item.title: Tools enable and constrain what we do with products
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I'm picking up from Boris Müller's premise in his essay Tools Shape Our Products where tools can contribute to innovation and the creation of things that would be impossible with our hands alone. But he also explores how the tools we use shape the way we think about solutions. Müller traces a path t…

item.title: Crypto, Its Promises, and the Design Challenges
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Hello everyone, after 3 weeks without sending anything, I allowed myself a break to finish reading the Token Economy book. The idea is to round out the ideas I sketched in editions 6 and 16 of this newsletter, and reflect in writing, as usual, on what I learned.

item.title: Decentralization vs Centralization
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: This has been one of the editions I've had to rewrite the most, and it ended up being the longest. A very special thanks to Daniel Gurrola and Yessica Avila, who helped me bounce my ideas around—ideas that were clear in my head but confusing when I wrote them down.

item.title: NFTs: Blockchain Applications in the Real World
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few months ago I got a text from one of my friends. He sent me a link to a video showing how easy it was to create an NFT from an image and sell it. Beyond the technical stuff, my first question was, Why would anyone buy an image that's an NFT, and what gives it value?

item.title: Decentralized Society (DeSoc) with Soulbound Tokens
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Just a few weeks ago Decentralized Society: Finding Web3's Soul1 was published, where E. Glen Weyl, Puja Ohlhaver and Vitalik Buterin discuss a new type of non-transferable token that could change the rules of the game.

item.title: Good design is about process, not the product
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I was away for almost two months, and the goal of having our 52 editions still looks far away, but sometimes what counts is not how much you advance; but simply that you advance.

item.title: Growth path: or how to never stop growing
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: People tend to think of career growth as something linear and always aimed at higher positions in the hierarchy. In Design, the last few years this growth has happened aggressively, because companies are starting to adopt a design mindset, teams scale quickly and designers have the opportunity to gr…

item.title: Apple's Dynamic Island on iPhone 14
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: This might be one of those editions where I write about a timely topic, or rather a trending one. It's not the first time—my posts about W3 and tokens are proof of that.

item.title: Figma is Acquired by Adobe
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: By the time this edition comes out, it will have been a week since the announcement that Figma is acquired by Adobe. The news became a trending topic in design circles, displeasure was the general response, and the expectations of current Creative Suite users plus the memes make Adobe's status cryst…

item.title: On Success and Innovation
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I've finished "Tim Cook's Apple" and I've had a moment of reflection about what success and innovation really mean.

item.title: The Tangible Nature of the Intangible
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I must say that in a box under my desk, sealed tight to keep out the dust, there's a CD collection I built during my teenage years and college days. And also, on a hard drive, I have about 30 GBs of MP3s treasured as my music collection, all properly named and with their respective album art.

item.title: Why Adobe needs to “Figmatize” and not the other way around
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A couple of weeks ago, I watched the Briar Levit documentary titled “Graphic Means”, which talks about the history of graphic design, from linotypes to photo compositions, and finally, the Pasteup, the analog version of the current digital graphic design.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence in the Creative Industries
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: For several months now, almost a year, the boom of Artificial Intelligences (from now on AIs) that can recreate images has been trending. Dall-E, Midjourney have made headlines with their results. Cosmopolitan became the first magazine to put one of these creations on its cover and even an AI-made a…

item.title: Creating tomorrow by executing today
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Now that I'm in a calm work environment, it's easy to understand the importance of having moments of stillness. I tend to be pragmatic, looking for tangible and feasible solutions; however, I've come to understand again that it's also worthwhile to see everything from a more macro perspective, freei…

item.title: "Take Power and Give It to the People": Has the Promise of the Internet Been Fulfilled?
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: After watching The Playlist, the story of how Spotify was founded on Netflix, I've been reflecting on what these paradigm shifts and the digitization of services have meant. As I mentioned in edition 28: What matters is no longer owning it, now it's: watching it whenever and wherever you want.

item.title: The goal of UI is not to be pretty and pleasant
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I've always believed that graphic designers, visual artists, and those who work in creative industries have a tendency to seek the spotlight, even indirectly. Maybe not all of us like being present when people talk about our work, but we like people talking about our work.

item.title: Resilience — That's What Design Is About
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: 2022 has brought us surprises. The year is ending very differently from how it started. Uncertainty has become part of the landscape.

item.title: Before 2023 Begins
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: If I'm honest, I didn't imagine myself writing about my newsletter a year later, and here we are.

item.title: Faster Ideation Processes with AI
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Microsoft and OpenAI's latest moves have put pressure on Google, and it seems things haven't gone well at all with Bard.

item.title: Asking for feedback in design
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few days ago I had this reflection after exchanging ideas on WhatsApp in a group where UI topics are discussed (which Adrián Solca opened as part of the UX en Serio community).

item.title: Ed. 39
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Things don't work because they're beautiful, they're beautiful because they work

item.title: Prompt Designer: Confusing a Possible Skill with a Nonexistent Role.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: In this quest to understand the Next Big Thing, we want to do some "foresight" on how AI (Artificial Intelligence) will impact our work as designers. Overwhelmed by the uncertainty of not knowing how much and how what we do can be automated: we future-cast.

item.title: Design Education: Schools, Bootcamps, and Practice.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: For those who don't know, I studied Design and Visual Communication at what is now the Faculty of Arts and Design (UNAM) in Mexico. But the truth is that much of what I do today wasn't taught to me in my degree program. Back then, people said "Multimedia" when referring to web, people used CDs and D…

item.title: What Tony Fadell's book taught me about product design
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I devoured Build, Tony's book (also available in Spanish). He has this gift for telling stories, for sharing how his life has been while simultaneously guiding you and teaching you about product design and the teams that make those things possible.

item.title: Fidelity Levels in Design Artifacts
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I've mentioned this in the previous edition, Design implies being able to communicate ideas effectively. If people can't understand what you communicate, they won't be able to understand how you made decisions.

item.title: When Artificial Intelligence Catches Up to Us.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: The latest Adobe Photoshop Beta is without a doubt the utopia of any designer who works in digital graphic editing. What used to be memes, impossible things that only made sense as jokes, are now our closest reality.

item.title: From Metaverse to Spatial Computing
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: That was the only thing going through my head while watching WWDC23 when I realized what Apple was announcing was a headset…

item.title: The best WWDC videos about interface design
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Today I'll share a very personal reflection. It's not about design, but it is about my view on life and our purpose.

item.title: My Version of Desk Research for UI and Interaction Designers.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: As I'm finishing up this edition, there are 208 subscribers to Iterative Thinking. Thank you so much, and to the newcomers—welcome!

item.title: Tools: Biases and Enablers of Design
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Before we begin, I want to welcome all the new subscribers and give a special thank you to UXBS from my friend Cristobal. I know many of you who subscribed to this newsletter in recent weeks did so from his recommendation. If you're not following the UXBS newsletter, I can only tell you that you're …

item.title: Design Automaton
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: The practice of design is refined over time and through practice, we already discussed this in edition 14 of this newsletter. Your theoretical and academic foundation is just a starting point. Experience is forged by designing — otherwise we end up being design theorists but not designers.

item.title: The trap of "common sense" and "intuition" in design.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: This edition marks a very important milestone. Originally when I started this newsletter I wanted to write 52 editions in a year (one per week) and it took me 8 extra months to achieve it. However, 52 was a number that seemed far away and now it has been reached. Thanks to everyone who has been read…

item.title: 4 Things Tony Fadell's Build Taught Me About Team Design
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Second post about Tony Fadell's "Build." You can read my first post about this book here.

item.title: Documentary: RIP! A Remix Manifesto
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few days ago, I responded to a post that claimed design is no longer design, that standardization has killed the space for creativity. And here I explain better why I don't believe that…

item.title: On When Graphic Designers Hijacked the UX Industry
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Many old-guard UX Designers, or those who don't come from a visual background, have expressed the idea that when visual and graphic designers entered the industry, it was hijacked by designers focused only on UI, who without understanding much about the UCD and UX process jumped into designing solut…

item.title: For 2024
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: It's been a little over two months since the last edition of this newsletter. Several health problems in my family in November made December a month for catching up on pending things, and aside from video editing for the podcast and some interactions on social media, the truth is I preferred to rech…

item.title: InVision: The End of an Era of Design Audiovisual Content.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few days ago we learned that InVision is shutting down and for many nostalgics (myself included), memories came flooding back; because many of us used that tool at some point.

item.title: Design Systems and the Challenges of Collaboration
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: The advances in visual creation that artificial intelligence is offering pose a paradigm shift for the designer as craftsman, changing their way of working in a very radical way. Perhaps it's much more appropriate to talk about the designer as curator or as a decision factor facing an Artificial Int…

item.title: Sharing what you know makes you a better designer
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Hi, I've been a bit absent because 2024 is a year where I'll be teaching several courses, so because of the planning involved I've prioritized reading and rereading a lot of material related to Design Systems, Interface Design, Interaction, and Prototyping. I must confess I've had to put my urge to …

item.title: Design needs a premise
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: And there we are, in that moment of initiation, where we only have the blank page, we close our eyes as if our interior could give us solutions, we breathe and contemplate the blank page, and we freeze… the blank page moment paralyzes us.

item.title: Passion and a taste for UX.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I remember that day very well. I was filling out the paperwork to study at university, and my first choice was Design and Visual Communication. My brother, 10 years older, told me Design wasn't that hard because it didn't have courses with many credits. He's an actuary, and at the time he was tellin…

item.title: Demystifying UX Industry Facts.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: In the design world, you often hear the phrase "UX is not UI." I've already written my position on this. The distinction works well for understanding roles, delimiting responsibilities, and even skills. But it's no longer convenient in a strict sense to view UI as something external to the UX proces…

item.title: Figma and AI: What Are We Really Looking For?
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A little over a week since Figma's Config Event took place. It's curious that although it's a forum for reflecting on the state of the industry, best practices in digital product design, and new paradigms in design like artificial intelligence or design system scaling, the Hype centered on the openi…

item.title: 2024 Retrospective
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: The truth is I've asked myself many times: What do I write about in the next Iterative Thinking edition? I haven't read something that connects the dots to create an aha moment or a eureka to write about, and if we add that I've been reading less, things get worse.

item.title: AI designer. The market's obsession with labels
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: More than a year ago I predicted that the "Prompt Designer" role wouldn't have much of a future as a specialization within design. Since then, we haven't seen this role flourish.

item.title: Figma with UI3, and designers showing their resistance to change
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I hate by the book. For years I've understood that what theory says is more of a framework than something to take as recipes. Sure, it's valid to learn from recipes at the beginning, but with time and as we develop more experience, we realize that reality has the bad habit of having nuances—we'll ne…

item.title: "Good Taste" in Design Doesn't Exist
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: A few weeks ago I read a LinkedIn post saying that designers need to develop good taste, that knowing how to use Figma isn't enough, you need to spend hours developing your eye and with it the good taste to create quality designs.

item.title: Reevaluating Success in the Social Media Era
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: As we approach the end of the year, it's natural to do a retrospective on our goals. However, I firmly believe that any moment is valid to look back and reflect on what we like and what we want to change.

item.title: Not Everything Fits in a Design System: Not Even If You Know How to Make It Fit, Trust Me!
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: This week I just finished the third edition of my Edison course — and by the way, the cohort was really great, and I had a blast with the kinds of questions they had, bringing freshness and dynamism to each session. Some of the recurring questions inspired this post.

item.title: When Design Gets Automated: What's Left for Designers?
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: It's evident that, as designers, our way of working has experienced significant changes. The pandemic put many services to the test and showed us it was possible to run processes remotely. Collaborative tools gained new value by letting us work in parallel and optimize our workflows.

item.title: Design is not what you see, it's how you get there
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Design is an activity in which expertise develops through practice over time.

item.title: Vibe Designing: when we let AI decide for us.
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Automating design processes with Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer a future possibility—it's already happening. Jakob Nielsen anticipates that designers of the future will be more orchestrators than implementers: we'll think more about what needs to be done than how to do it. In essence, we'…

item.title: Automating is not the same as understanding
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: The promise of automation has been with us long before artificial intelligence existed. From washing machines to Excel macros, we've trusted technology to reduce effort on repetitive tasks and free up human energy for more important things.

item.title: Delegating Tasks with AI in Experience Design
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: It's undeniable how the collaboration paradigm has been changing. Integrating AI into our workflow can be exciting, but we also need to be transparent: in many areas, AI still promises more than it delivers, expectations create hype that in many cases is just noise.

item.title: Expertise: How AI Reorganizes Which Types of Work Become Valuable
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: In our previous edition we explored practical strategies for delegating tasks to artificial intelligence. We talked about how to identify which activities to hand off to automated tools and which to keep under human control to maximize productivity without sacrificing quality.

item.title: How to Make AI Elevate Your Value (Instead of Making You Replaceable)
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: In our previous edition we explored the Expertise framework from MIT that reveals how automation can simultaneously elevate or degrade professional value, depending on which tasks we decide to automate.

item.title: Vibe Coding: How Building an App in a Weekend Taught Me Why AI Won't Replace Us
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: I had a CSV file with over 800 links saved across almost eight years.

item.title: The problem isn't AI, it's how we simplify reality
item.author: ulises@iterativethinking.com (Ulises Arvizu)
item.description: Apologies for the months without writing. Between taking a good break at the end of the year and 2026 starting with many changes—moving and other news—I couldn't find the time to put certain ideas in order. But here we are, back again.
